title: Fabric led
abstract: An improves fabric LED and process for coating conductive material onto the fabric and thereby knitting the fabric into a fabric LED. The fabric can be initially coated with at least two conductive materials such as, a conductive polymer in order to form at least two conductive fabric materials. The conductive fabric materials can be formed by treating the fabric/yarn with a liquid/vapour state conductive polymer and fusing the conductive material the fabric. The conductive fabric material can be further knitted with non-conductive fabric alternativley/ at a specific defined pattern to form a conductive matrix array. The intersection between two conductive fabrics in the contains an electroluminescent material that emits light when a charge is applied in order to thereby form a fabric LED.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  I/We Claim: 1. A process of coating and knitting a fabric LED, said process comprising: coating fabric with at least two conductive materials in order to form at least two conductive fabric materials wherein the conductive fabric materials can be formed by treating the fabric/yarn with a liquid/vapor state conductive polymer and fusing the conductive material with the fabric. knitting the conductive fabric material with non-conductive fabric at a specific defined pattern to form a conductive matrix array wherein the intersection between two conductive fabrics in the matrix 1 contains an electroluminescent material that emits light when a charge is applied in order to thereby form a fabric LED. 2. The process of claim 1 further comprising connecting the array elements of the conductive fabric matrix to a micro controller unit which thereby controls the charge applied on the electroluminescent materials of the conductive fabric matrix in order thereby produce various display patterns. 3. The process of claim 1 further comprising connecting the micro controller unit to a programmable processing unit which enables the user to transmit the intent of an action on the electroluminescent materials of the fabric LED. 4. The process of claim 1 further comprising displaying the user intended/customized content on fabric LED matrix based on the inputs from a processing unit wherein the processing unit stores programmable intended actions/commands of the user and thereby submits relevant commands to the micro controller for displaying intended content of the user. 5. The process of claim 1 further comprising storing the user intended/customized content on programmable processing unit and thereby controlling and managing the content on fabric LED matrix. 6. The process of claim 1 further comprising receiving the user intended/customized content at the programmable processing unit from a control application of a data processing unit. 7. The process of claim 1 further comprising connecting micro controller 

 unit associated with the fabric conductive matrix to the programmable processing unit via a wireless communication unit. 8. The process of claim 1 wherein programmable processing unit can be a microprocessor unit wherein said microprocessor unit stores and communicates the user intended/customized content with micro controller via a wireless communication unit. 9. The process of claim 7 wherein the wireless communication unit works in tandem with the processing unit to fetch the patterns or texts from the library that is stored on the control application and temporarily store the fetched patterns and texts in the processing unit. 10. The process of claim 1 wherein the conductive materials coated on the fabric can be a conductive polymer.
